questions.q: Bahadur Shah Zafar holds the distinction of being which figure in history?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last emperor of the Mughal dynasty.

questions.q: Apart from being an emperor, Bahadur Shah Zafar was renowned for which artistic pursuit?
questions.explanation: He was a gifted Urdu poet, writing under the pen name 'Zafar'.

questions.q: During the 1857 revolt, Bahadur Shah Zafar was proclaimed the symbolic leader of the rebels in which city?
questions.explanation: The rebels rallied around him as their symbolic leader in Delhi.

questions.q: After the defeat of the 1857 revolt, where was Bahadur Shah Zafar exiled by the British?
questions.explanation: He was exiled to Rangoon in Burma (modern Myanmar).

questions.q: In which year did Bahadur Shah Zafar die in exile?
questions.explanation: He died in Rangoon in 1862.

questions.q: The death of Bahadur Shah Zafar in exile marked the end of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: His death ended the Mughal dynasty, which had ruled large parts of India for over three centuries.

questions.q: How did Bahadur Shah Zafar leave the throne after the revolt was crushed?
options: He was crowned king of Burma
He was tried by the British and exiled
questions.explanation: After the revolt's defeat he was tried by the British and exiled.

questions.q: Bahadur Shah Zafar's role in 1857 made him a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: As the figurehead of the revolt, he became a symbol of Indian resistance to colonial rule.

questions.q: In which year did the revolt that elevated Bahadur Shah Zafar as a symbolic leader take place?
questions.explanation: The revolt in which he became the rebels' symbolic head occurred in 1857.

questions.q: What was Bahadur Shah Zafar's pen name as a poet?
questions.explanation: He composed verse under the takhallus (pen name) 'Zafar', meaning victory.

questions.q: What was Bahadur Shah Zafar's full regnal name as emperor?
questions.explanation: He reigned as Bahadur Shah II.

questions.q: Who was the father of Bahadur Shah Zafar, the Mughal emperor he succeeded?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar succeeded his father Akbar Shah II in 1837.

questions.q: By the time of the 1857 revolt, what was the real extent of Bahadur Shah Zafar's actual authority?
options: He ruled all of India directly
He commanded a vast standing army
His authority was largely confined to the Red Fort and Delhi area
questions.explanation: His effective authority had shrunk to little more than the Red Fort and the immediate Delhi region, with the British holding real power.

questions.q: In which year did Bahadur Shah Zafar ascend the Mughal throne?
questions.explanation: He became emperor in 1837 upon the death of his father.

questions.q: Which celebrated Urdu poet served at Bahadur Shah Zafar's court in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Mirza Ghalib was among the great poets associated with Zafar's Delhi court.

questions.q: When rebel sepoys from Meerut arrived in Delhi in May 1857, what did they demand of Bahadur Shah Zafar?
options: That he surrender to the British
That he lead and bless their revolt
That he disband the Mughal court
questions.explanation: The sepoys urged the reluctant emperor to become the head and symbol of their uprising.

questions.q: Where was Bahadur Shah Zafar found and captured by the British after the fall of Delhi in 1857?
questions.explanation: He took refuge at Humayun's Tomb, where he was captured by Major William Hodson.

questions.q: Which British officer captured Bahadur Shah Zafar and is associated with the killing of his sons?
questions.explanation: Major William Hodson captured Zafar and shot his sons near the Khooni Darwaza in Delhi.

questions.q: Bahadur Shah Zafar's famous lament in exile expressed sorrow that he could not get what in his homeland?
options: Two yards of land for burial
questions.explanation: His celebrated couplet mourns that he was so unlucky he could not even get two yards of land for burial in his beloved land.

questions.q: Which queen, the chief consort of Bahadur Shah Zafar, was prominent at the Mughal court during the revolt?
questions.explanation: Begum Zeenat Mahal was Zafar's influential consort and accompanied him into exile.

questions.q: What was the outcome of the British trial of Bahadur Shah Zafar in 1858?
options: He was sentenced to death and hanged
He was convicted and sentenced to exile
He was restored to the throne
questions.explanation: He was found guilty of rebellion and sentenced to exile, his life spared under the terms of his surrender.

questions.q: What was Bahadur Shah Zafar's approximate age when he was thrust into leadership of the 1857 revolt?
questions.explanation: Born in 1775, he was an elderly man in his early eighties at the time of the 1857 revolt.

questions.q: In what year was Bahadur Shah Zafar born?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar was born on 24 October 1775.

questions.q: Near which Delhi gateway were Bahadur Shah Zafar's sons shot by Major Hodson?
questions.explanation: His sons were killed near the gate later remembered as the Khooni Darwaza, or Bloody Gate.

questions.q: For many decades the exact site of Bahadur Shah Zafar's grave in Rangoon was lost, until it was rediscovered in which year?
questions.explanation: The buried grave was rediscovered in 1991 during excavation work near his shrine (dargah) in Yangon.

questions.q: Which son of Bahadur Shah Zafar served as a leading rebel commander at Delhi during the 1857 revolt?
questions.explanation: Zafar's son Mirza Mughal acted as a rebel commander at Delhi. Birjis Qadr, the boy-king proclaimed at Lucknow, was the son of Begum Hazrat Mahal, not of Zafar.

questions.q: William Dalrymple's acclaimed history centred on Bahadur Shah Zafar and 1857 carries which title?
questions.explanation: William Dalrymple's 'The Last Mughal' (2006) chronicles Zafar and the fall of Delhi in 1857.

questions.q: In which Mughal monument in Delhi did Bahadur Shah Zafar hold his court before the revolt?
questions.explanation: He held court at the Red Fort in Delhi, the seat of the Mughal emperors.

questions.q: What guarantee did Major Hodson give Bahadur Shah Zafar to secure his surrender at Humayun's Tomb?
options: That he would be restored to the throne
That he could return to Persia
That his lands would be returned
That his life would be spared
questions.explanation: Hodson promised that the emperor's life would be spared in exchange for his surrender, a pledge that determined the sentence of exile rather than execution.

questions.q: Bahadur Shah Zafar's tomb in Yangon is today venerated as what?
questions.explanation: His resting place in Yangon, Myanmar, is revered as a dargah and visited by pilgrims.
